Class Format

java.lang.Object
recoder.convenience.Format
public class Format extends Object
Create textual descriptions of program elements, program model elements or lists thereof. This class is useful to create readable information for error reports, debugging or model browsers.

  • Method Summary

    Method
    Description

  • Method Details

    • toString

      public static String toString(String formatText, ModelElement e)
      Replaces tags in the format strings with information pieces about the given program element, and returns it.
      Tag Replacement
      %n (Short) name of the entity, if available. Applies to named model elements, identifiers and compilation units. For compilation units, returns the short name of the primary type.
      %N Full name or path of the entity, if available. Applies to program model elements, named reference prefixes, identifiers and compilation units; works as %n for all other named model elements; methods have their signature attached using "%N" format; returns the canonical name of a compilation unit (equals the long name of the primary type).
      %m Name of the entity, if available. Applies to named model elements, identifiers and compilation units; works as "%n" except for methods, which have their signature attached using "%N" format.
      %s Trimmed source code, if available. Applies only to source elements.
      %c Unqualified class name of the object.
      %C Fully qualified class name of the object.
      %i Object id as obtained by System.identityHashCode().
      %p Source line/column start position from last parsing, if available (only for source elements).
      %P Source line/column start and end position from last parsing, if available (only for source elements).
      %r Relative source line/column indentation, if available (only for source elements).
      %u Compilation unit, logical name, if available. Applies only to program elements that have a compilation unit.
      %f File as obtained by DataLocation.getName(), if available. Applies only to program elements that have a compilation unit associated with a file).
      Any tag that does not apply does not produce an output (and is omitted, effectively).

      Since 0.72, the method allows to use a single digit in front of each qualifier, which controls minimum indentation. The digit is used for %p,%P, and %r.

      Parameters:
      formatText - the format text, containing tags.
      e - the model element to be formatted.
      Returns:
      a textual representation of the model element.

    • toString

      public static String toString(String formatText, List<? extends ModelElement> l)
      Formats a list of model elements. Each element is formatted according to the format string by a call to toString(String, String, String, String, List) using "(", ", ", ")" formatting.
      Parameters:
      formatText - the format text, containing tags.
      l - the list to be formatted.
      Returns:
      a textual representation of the list.
      See Also:
      • toString(String, String, String, String, List)

    • toString

      public static String toString(String formatText, String header, String separator, String footer, List<? extends ModelElement> l)
      Formats a list of model elements. Each element is formatted according to the format string, surrounded by the header/footer and each inner element is followed by a separator.
      Parameters:
      formatText - the format text, containing tags.
      header - the list start.
      separator - the element separator.
      footer - the list start.
      l - the list to be formatted.
      Returns:
      a textual representation of the list.
      Since:
      0.72

    • toString

      public static String toString(ProgramElement se)
      Formats a source element using a default format. The default format string is "\"%s\"@%p [%f]"
      Parameters:
      se - the source element to be formatted.
      Returns:
      a textual representation of the source element.

    • toString

      public static String toString(List<? extends ModelElement> l)
      Formats a program element list using a default format. The default format string is "\"%s\" @%p"
      Parameters:
      l - the list to be formatted.
      Returns:
      a textual representation of the list.
